gasChainId.description: Chain ID where gas is needed

requiresGas.description: Whether action requires gas (blockchain transaction fees)

optional.description:
Optional indicates if the connector can be omitted when required by an action.
If true, workflow nodes can have a null connector and the action will execute
without connector data (b3os-actions handles fallback if needed).

provider.description:
Provider indicates the underlying provider for this connector.
For Pipedream connectors, this is "pipedream".

providerConfig.description:
ProviderConfig contains provider-specific configuration.
For Pipedream connectors, this includes appSlug, authPropName, authType.

setupGuide.description: SetupGuide contains markdown instructions for setting up the connector.

nameTemplate.description: Display name with {{field}} interpolation (uses result field names, i.e. left side of FieldMapping)

github_com_b3-fun_b3-mono_services_b3os-.description: nil if action doesn't need connector

contractCode.description: Main contract Solidity source (flattened or with imports)

files.description:
Multi-file project support
Key: relative path (e.g., "src/interfaces/IERC20.sol")
Value: file content

verificationUrl.description: URL to view verified contract on block explorer

verified.description: Whether contract source is verified on block explorer

github_com_b3-fun_b3-mono_services_b3os-.description:
Deployments is populated from the contract_deployments table, NOT from a DB column.
Kept for backward-compatible JSON serialization in API responses.

orgStores.description: Named store snapshots: storeName → key → value

input.description: Input payload at time of completion/failure (may be raw or resolved)

iterations.description:
For for-each nodes only: tracks execution state of each iteration.
Each iteration is a mini ExecutionState graph (nodeID → ExecutionNode).

secretKeys.description: Input keys that contain connector secrets (for masking)

x-enum-comments.ExecutionNodeStatusCancelled: Set when run is cancelled by user
x-enum-comments.ExecutionNodeStatusPending: Set when run is created (all non-root nodes start as pending)
x-enum-comments.ExecutionNodeStatusScheduled: Set when node is scheduled for future execution
x-enum-comments.ExecutionNodeStatusSkipped: Set when node is skipped (e.g., non-taken if branch)
x-enum-comments.ExecutionNodeStatusSuccess: Set when node execution completes successfully
x-enum-comments.ExecutionNodeStatusWaiting: Set when node is paused waiting for a trigger

parentNodeId.description:
Legacy fields kept for backward-compatible JSON deserialization.
New code should only use Stack.

stack.description:
Stack holds nesting levels from outermost to innermost.
Single-level for-each uses a stack of length 1.

bucketStart.description: Start of time bucket (hourly or daily)

default.description: Default value (used if not provided at runtime)

type.description: string, number, integer, boolean, array, object

chainPropKey.description: For tokenSelector: prop key providing chainId

inputType.description:
InputType is a UI rendering hint. The frontend uses it to pick a rich
selector component instead of a plain text input. Unknown values are
silently ignored (falls back to default input).
Valid values: chainSelector, tokenSelector, address, recipientAddress, email

buttonLabel.description: ButtonLabel is the text displayed on the trigger button

description.description: Description explains what the workflow does

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the public widget is active

maxAmount.description: MaxAmount is the maximum amount allowed per trigger (in smallest unit)

minAmount.description: MinAmount is the minimum amount required to trigger (in smallest unit)

title.description: Title is the display title for the widget

x-enum-comments.RunStatusWaiting: Run is paused, waiting for a trigger to resume

count.description: Scope-dependent: template, action, trigger, or connector count

organizationId.description: Fields for user-generated templates (nil for built-in templates)

sourceWorkflowId.description: Source workflow link (nil for built-in or legacy templates)

bucketStart.description: Start of time bucket (hourly or daily)

usageCount.description: Number of executions in this bucket

hasDraft.description:
Response-only fields (not persisted to database)
HasDraft indicates a draft exists (for live workflows in API responses)

hasLiveVersion.description: HasLiveVersion indicates a live version exists (for drafts in API responses)

github_com_b3-fun_b3-mono_services_b3os-.description:
Definition is the full workflow graph to execute. Must contain a
"root" node with type "manual". All action nodes referenced in
children arrays must be present.

branch.description: "then" or "else", only when parent is "if" node

loopBody.description: Loop body nodes (only for for-each nodes)

resultSchema.description:
ResultSchema describes the output structure of this node (populated dynamically for x402 endpoints).
This is used by the frontend to show available output variables in the variable picker.

titleOverride.description: AI-generated short title (2-6 words), only when more descriptive than default

chainId.description: Chain ID where payment should be made (e.g., 8453 for Base)

discoverable.description:
Discoverable controls whether this workflow is listed in the Bazaar public catalog.
Bazaar is a discovery marketplace where users can find and pay for x402-enabled endpoints.
When true:  Endpoint is publicly listed, anyone can trigger with payment only (no secret needed).
When false: Endpoint is private, requires both secret and payment to trigger.

price.description: Price in the smallest unit of the token (e.g., wei for ETH, 6 decimals for USDC)

recipient.description: Recipient address that will receive the payment

description.description: Description provides details about what the workflow does

logo.description: Logo URL for the workflow/provider logo

name.description: Name is the display name for the workflow

provider.description: Provider is the name of the entity providing this workflow

example.description: Example provides a sample value for documentation and testing

schema.description: Schema is a JSON Schema definition for validation

baseUrl.description:
=== ENDPOINT IDENTITY ===
BaseURL is the clean URL without query params (e.g., "https://api.example.com/search")

defaultQueryParams.description: Default values (applied if user doesn't provide at runtime)

queryParams.description:
=== INPUT CONFIGURATION ===
CDP-aligned flat structure: {"paramName": {"type": "string", "description": "...", "required": true}}

x-enum-comments.X402EndpointSourceCDPBazaar: Imported from CDP Bazaar discovery API

github_com_b3-fun_b3-mono_services_b3os-.description: OutputSchema for discovery registration (matches TypeScript SDK format)

body.description: Body contains JSON Schema for body parameters (used by some x402 providers)

bodyFields.description: BodyFields contains body field definitions in alternative CDP format

bodyParams.description: BodyParams contains body param definitions in CDP format

query.description: Query contains JSON Schema for query parameters (used by some x402 providers)

queryParams.description: QueryParams contains query param definitions in CDP format

chainId.description: ChainID is the chain ID for EIP-712 domain (from quote)

facilitatorAddress.description: FacilitatorAddress is the facilitator's address that will be approved as spender

name.description: Name is the ERC20 token name (used in EIP-712 domain)

signatureType.description: SignatureType specifies the signature method: "authorization" (EIP-3009) or "permit" (ERC-2612)

verifyingContract.description: VerifyingContract is the token contract address for EIP-712 domain (from quote)

version.description: Version is the ERC20 token version (used in EIP-712 domain)

srcTokenAddress.description: Cross-token payment fields (set when buyer uses X-PREFERRED-TOKEN)

isActive.description: Pointer to distinguish false from unset

isRequired.description: Pointer to distinguish false from unset

failedRunId.description: Optional - ID of a failed run to provide error context for debugging

images.description: Optional - images attached to the message (base64 for LLM, IPFS for storage)

lastEventIndex.description: Last received event index for replay (reconnection)

message.description: Optional on reconnect, required for new sessions

preferredMode.description: Optional - user's preferred mode hint; overrides auto-classifier when set

requestId.description: Client-generated UUID4 for persistent session tracking (optional for legacy/inline mode)

sessionId.description: Optional - for create page (temporary session context)

timezone.description: Optional - user's IANA timezone (e.g. "America/New_York") for time-aware responses

workflowId.description: Optional - for edit page (existing workflow context)

contextType.description: ContextType indicates whether this is a "session" or "workflow" chat

firstMessageAt.description: FirstMessageAt is when the first message was sent

lastMessageAt.description: LastMessageAt is when the last message was sent

messageCount.description: MessageCount is the total number of messages in this session

preview.description: Preview is the content of the first user message (truncated)

sessionId.description: SessionID for create-page sessions (client-generated, e.g., session_1234567890_abc)

workflowName.description: WorkflowName is the name of the associated workflow (if available)

contractId.description: ContractID is the primary identifier (optional for first message - backend creates project)

key.description: Key is the full plaintext API key, shown only once. Store it securely.

sessionId.description: Optional: migrate chat history from session to workflow

templateId.description: Optional: template ID to track fork count

templateProps.description: Optional: template prop schema (for copy-to-org with sanitized props)

defaultQueryParams.description: Default values (applied if user doesn't provide at runtime)

queryParams.description:
Input configuration (CDP-aligned flat structure)
Format: {"paramName": {"type": "string", "description": "...", "required": true}}

payload.description:
Payload is merged into the trigger result (root node output),
accessible via {{root.result.key}} expressions. A "triggeredAt"
timestamp is always added automatically.

propValues.description:
PropValues are template parameter values injected into the run
context, accessible via {{$props.key}} expressions.

runId.description:
RunID is an optional client-generated run ID. Must start with
"run_" prefix. If omitted, a run ID is generated server-side.
Use this to subscribe to SSE stream before triggering the run.

description.description: Description is a short summary of what the workflow does.

name.description: Name is the display name of the workflow.

workflowName.description: WorkflowName is the display name of the workflow

name.description: Optional: User can override AI-generated values

walletAddress.description:
Optional: Specify which wallet to use for payment.
If not provided, uses the organization's default wallet.

body.description:
Optional body to send with the probe request.
Some endpoints require valid input before returning 402.

method.description:
Optional HTTP method to use. If not specified, tries POST then GET.
Valid values: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE

PublicWidgetData.description: Widget contains the public widget configuration with derived trigger values

buttonLabel.description: ButtonLabel is the text displayed on the trigger button

chainId.description: ChainID is derived from the trigger's chainId

description.description: Description explains what the workflow does

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether the public widget is active

maxAmount.description: MaxAmount is the maximum amount allowed per trigger (in smallest unit)

minAmount.description: MinAmount is the minimum amount required to trigger (in smallest unit)

recipientAddress.description: RecipientAddress is derived from the trigger's toAddress/contractAddress

title.description: Title is the display title for the widget

tokenAddress.description: TokenAddress is derived from the trigger's tokenAddress (empty for native token)

buttonLabel.description: ButtonLabel is the text displayed on the trigger button (optional, defaults to "Send")

description.description: Description explains what the workflow does (optional)

maxAmount.description: MaxAmount is the maximum amount allowed per trigger (optional)

minAmount.description: MinAmount is the minimum amount required to trigger (optional)

title.description: Title is the display title for the widget (required)

runId.description:
Optional run ID. If provided, must start with "run_" prefix.
Use this to subscribe to SSE stream before triggering the run.

version.description:
Optional version number. When set, run this specific version instead of
the default (which prioritizes draft). Useful for testing the live version
while a draft exists.

transaction.description: May be null if server doesn't return it

type.description: name, nodes, root, connectivity, pattern, variable

contractAddress.description: Optional - uses address from deployment if not provided

triggerType.description: Pre-extracted fields (avoid FE parsing full definition).
